risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to uneven heat distribution.
risk.mit: Implement enhanced heat dissipation channels and ensure uniform thermal conductivity materials.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure under unexpected launch loads.
risk.mit: Conduct additional structural integrity tests beyond MIL-STD-810G requirements.

risk.desc: Insufficient clearance for moving parts may cause jamming.
risk.mit: Review and adjust clearances for all moving interfaces to ensure smooth operation.

risk.desc: Inconsistent manufacturing tolerances could affect assembly.
risk.mit: Standardize manufacturing processes and conduct regular quality checks.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to non-standard fasteners.
risk.mit: Use standard fasteners and ensure all mating features comply with industry standards.

generated_parts.label: Payload Optics Bench
generated_parts.spec: EO payload optics bench, thermal stability, optical baffles, focal plane interface, lens mounts

requirements: EO payload optics bench, thermal stability, optical baffles, focal plane interface, lens mounts
Removable payload module: optics bench, focal plane, thermal baffles, inspection window
Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Rails, PC/104 standoffs, payload bay, and board stack clearances are visible
Avionics, battery, antenna, and payload modules have removable trays or access panels
Design is an educational structural/electrical mockup and avoids flight qualification claims
Structure, power, avionics, comms, and payload are decomposed with simple mass/volume/power budget notes per module that sum consistently

verify.rule: All required fields are present and non-empty.
The estimated mass matches the expected mass.
Each required evidence feature has corresponding geometry operations.
Interfaces with adjacent modules are reciprocal and clearly defined.
Critical joints have visible locking or preload evidence.

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion mismatch between Al 7075 and CFRP materials.
risk.mit: Conduct thermal cycling tests to validate material compatibility.

risk.desc: Inspection window may obstruct optical alignment if not properly positioned.
risk.mit: Review optical path and adjust inspection window location if necessary.

risk.desc: Strain relief channels may not provide adequate protection if improperly routed.
risk.mit: Ensure all cables follow specified routing paths with proper strain relief.

risk.desc: Connector accessibility may be compromised if not designed with sufficient clearance.
risk.mit: Verify connector placement and clearance during assembly design review.

risk.desc: Mounting datum for Power Distribution Plate may not align perfectly due to manufacturing tolerances.
risk.mit: Include tolerance analysis and adjust assembly order hint if necessary.

risk.mit: Implement additional thermal straps or insulating layers as needed.

risk.desc: Insufficient structural support for the radiator face could lead to deformation under launch loads.
risk.mit: Increase the thickness or add stiffeners to the radiator face.

risk.mit: Add fillets or chamfers to the frame mounting flange to reduce stress.

risk.desc: Sensor interface may not align properly with the electronics module connector.
